---
title: "Skill Gap Analysis"
format: html
---

## Overview

This section evaluates the gap between the skills required in the job market and the current skill levels of our team members. By comparing these two perspectives, we identify areas for improvement and provide actionable recommendations for career development.

---

## Team Skill Matrix

We assessed each team member’s proficiency across key technical skills using a scale from 1 (Beginner) to 5 (Expert).

```{python}
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv(
    "data/lightcast_job_postings.csv",
    on_bad_lines="skip",
    low_memory=False
)

skills_data = {
    "Name": ["Saori", "Caroolina", "Member3"],
    "Python": [2, 4, 2],
    "SQL": [2, 4, 2],
    "Machine Learning": [2, 4, 1],
    "Cloud Computing": [1, 4, 1],
    "Data Visualization": [2, 4, 2]
}

df_skills = pd.DataFrame(skills_data)
df_skills = df_skills.set_index("Name")
df_skills
```

```{python}
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
sns.heatmap(df_skills, annot=True, cmap="coolwarm", linewidths=0.5)
plt.title("Team Skill Levels Heatmap")
plt.show()
```

```{python}
from collections import Counter

if "SKILLS_NAME" in df.columns:
    skills = df["SKILLS_NAME"].dropna().str.split(";").explode()
    top_skills = skills.value_counts().head(10)

    plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
    top_skills.plot(kind="bar")
    plt.title("Top Skills in Job Market")
    plt.xlabel("Skills")
    plt.ylabel("Frequency")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45)
    plt.show()
```
